title: भस्मजाबाल उपनिषद् प्रमुख मन्त्र
devanagariText:
॥ अथ भस्मजाबालोपनिषद् प्रमुखमन्त्राः ॥

ॐ अग्निरिति भस्म। वायुरिति भस्म। जलमिति भस्म।
स्थलमिति भस्म। व्योमेति भस्म। सर्वं ह वा इदं भस्म।
मन एतानि चक्षूंषि भस्मानि॥१॥

ॐ त्र्यायुषं जमदग्नेः कश्यपस्य त्र्यायुषम्।
यद्देवेषु त्र्यायुषं तन्मेऽस्तु त्र्यायुषम्॥२॥

ॐ त्र्यम्बकं यजामहे सुगन्धिं पुष्टिवर्धनम्।
उर्वारुकमिव बन्धनान्मृत्योर्मुक्षीय मामृतात्॥३॥

ॐ ईशानः सर्वविद्यानामीश्वरः सर्वभूतानां
ब्रह्माधिपतिर्ब्रह्मणोऽधिपतिर्ब्रह्मा शिवो मे अस्तु सदाशिवोम्॥४॥

ॐ सद्योजातं प्रपद्यामि सद्योजाताय वै नमो नमः।
भवे भवे नातिभवे भवस्व मां भवोद्भवाय नमः॥५॥

ॐ वामदेवाय नमो ज्येष्ठाय नमः श्रेष्ठाय नमो
रुद्राय नमः कालाय नमः कलविकरणाय नमो
बलविकरणाय नमो बलाय नमो बलप्रमथनाय नमः
सर्वभूतदमनाय नमो मनोन्मनाय नमः॥६॥

ॐ अघोरेभ्योऽथ घोरेभ्यो घोरघोरतरेभ्यः।
सर्वेभ्यः सर्वशर्वेभ्यो नमस्ते अस्तु रुद्ररूपेभ्यः॥७॥

ॐ तत्पुरुषाय विद्महे महादेवाय धीमहि।
तन्नो रुद्रः प्रचोदयात्॥८॥

ॐ ईशानः सर्वविद्यानामीश्वरः सर्वभूतानाम्।
ब्रह्माधिपतिर्ब्रह्मणोऽधिपतिर्ब्रह्मा शिवो मे अस्तु सदाशिवोम्॥९॥

मृत्युञ्जयमन्त्रेण भस्मोद्धूलनं कुर्यात्।
ललाटे त्रिपुण्ड्रं धारयेत्।
ऊर्ध्वपुण्ड्रं वा त्रिपुण्ड्रं वा भस्मना विधिवत्।
भस्म हि शिवस्य दिव्यमूर्तिरस्ति॥१०॥

ॐ अग्निरिति भस्म वायुरिति भस्म जलमिति भस्म
स्थलमिति भस्म व्योमेति भस्म सर्वं ह वा इदं भस्म
मन एतानि चक्षूंषि भस्मानि।
यो भस्मनि तिष्ठन् भस्म न वेद यस्य भस्म
शरीरं यो भस्मन्तरो यमयति स त आत्मान्तर्याम्यमृतः॥११॥

॥ इति भस्मजाबालोपनिषद् प्रमुखमन्त्राः सम्पूर्णाः ॥
transliteration:
|| Atha Bhasma Jabala Upanishad Pramukha Mantraah ||

Om Agniriti Bhasma. Vaayuriti Bhasma. Jalamiti Bhasma.
Sthalamiti Bhasma. Vyometi Bhasma. Sarvam Ha Vaa Idam Bhasma.
Mana Etaani Chakshumshi Bhasmaani.||1||

Om Tryaayusham Jamadagneh Kashyapasya Tryaayusham.
Yaddeveshu Tryaayusham Tanme'stu Tryaayusham.||2||

Om Tryambakam Yajaamahe Sugandhim Pushtirvardhanam.
Urvaaarukamiva Bandhanaanmrityormuksheeya Maamritaat.||3||

Om Eeshaanah Sarvavidyaanaam-Eeshvarah Sarvabhootaanaam
Brahmaadhipatir-Brahmano'dhipatir-Brahmaa Shivo Me Astu Sadaashivom.||4||

Om Sadyojaatam Prapadyaami Sadyojaataaya Vai Namo Namah.
Bhave Bhave Naatibhave Bhavasva Maam Bhavodbhavaaya Namah.||5||

Om Vaamadevaaya Namo Jyeshthaaya Namah Shreshthaaya Namo
Rudraaya Namah Kaalaaya Namah Kalavikaranaaya Namo
Balavikaranaaya Namo Balaaya Namo Balapramathanaaya Namah
Sarvabhootadamanaaya Namo Manonmanaaya Namah.||6||

Om Aghorebhyo'tha Ghorebhyo Ghoraghoratarebhyah.
Sarvebhyah Sarvasharvebhyo Namaste Astu Rudraroopebhyah.||7||

Om Tatpurushaaya Vidmahe Mahadevaaya Dheemahi.
Tanno Rudrah Prachodayaat.||8||

Om Eeshaanah Sarvavidyaanaam-Eeshvarah Sarvabhootaanaam.
Brahmaadhipatir-Brahmano'dhipatir-Brahmaa Shivo Me Astu Sadaashivom.||9||

Mrityunjaya-Mantrena Bhasmoddhoolanam Kuryaat.
Lalaate Tripundram Dhaarayet.
Urdhvapundram Vaa Tripundram Vaa Bhasmanaa Vidhivat.
Bhasma Hi Shivasya Divyamoortirasti.||10||

Om Agniriti Bhasma Vaayuriti Bhasma Jalamiti Bhasma
Sthalamiti Bhasma Vyometi Bhasma Sarvam Ha Vaa Idam Bhasma
Mana Etaani Chakshumshi Bhasmaani.
Yo Bhasmani Tishthan Bhasma Na Veda Yasya Bhasma
Shareeram Yo Bhasmantaro Yamayati Sa Ta Aatmaantaryaamyamritah.||11||

|| Iti Bhasma Jabala Upanishad Pramukha Mantraah Sampoornaah ||
hindiMeaning:
१. अग्नि भस्म है, वायु भस्म है, जल भस्म है, स्थल भस्म है, आकाश भस्म है। यह सब कुछ भस्म ही है। मन और ये नेत्र भी भस्ममय हैं।

२. जमदग्नि का त्रयायुष, कश्यप का त्रयायुष, देवों में जो त्रयायुष है — वह मुझे प्राप्त हो।

३. हम त्र्यम्बक (तीन नेत्रों वाले शिव) का यजन करते हैं जो सुगन्धमय, पुष्टि-वर्धक हैं। जैसे ककड़ी बन्धन से मुक्त होती है, वैसे मृत्यु से मुक्त करें, अमृत से नहीं (अमृत बना रहे)।

४. ईशान सर्वविद्याओं के ईश्वर, सर्वभूतों के ईश, ब्रह्मा के अधिपति — वे शिव मुझे सदाशिव रूप में प्रसन्न हों।

५. मैं सद्योजात की शरण लेता हूँ, सद्योजात को बार-बार नमस्कार। भव-भव में अत्यन्त भव न हो, मुझमें प्रकट हों — भवोद्भव को नमस्कार।

६. वामदेव को नमस्कार, ज्येष्ठ को, श्रेष्ठ को, रुद्र को, काल को, कलविकरण को, बलविकरण को, बल को, बलप्रमथन को, सर्वभूतदमन को, मनोन्मन को नमस्कार।

७. अघोर (सौम्य), घोर (भयंकर), और घोर से भी अत्यन्त घोर — सब रुद्ररूपों को नमस्कार।

८. उस तत्पुरुष (महादेव) को हम जानते हैं, महादेव का ध्यान करते हैं। वे रुद्र हमें प्रेरित करें।

९. ईशान सर्वविद्याओं व सर्वभूतों के ईश्वर हैं — वे सदाशिव मुझे सुखी करें।

१०. मृत्युञ्जय मन्त्र से भस्म-उद्धूलन करे। ललाट पर त्रिपुण्ड्र धारण करे। ऊर्ध्वपुण्ड्र या त्रिपुण्ड्र भस्म से विधिवत् करे। भस्म शिव की दिव्य मूर्ति है।

११. (पुनः) सब कुछ भस्ममय है। जो भस्म में स्थित रहकर भस्म को नहीं जानता, जिसका शरीर भस्म है, जो भस्म के भीतर से नियन्त्रित करता है — वह तेरा आत्मा, अन्तर्यामी, अमृतस्वरूप है।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीbhasmajabalaupanishadkeymantrasमन्त्रस्य जाबालि ऋषिः। विविध (गद्यात्मक एवं अनुष्टुप्) छन्दः। श्री शिव (रुद्र) देवता। ॐ नमः शिवाय बीजम्। भस्म-शिव शक्तिः। भस्मधारण फल प्राप्ति कीलकम्। श्रीशिवप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Contains the sacred Bhasma (holy ash) mantras from the Atharvaveda Upanishad
Includes all five Panchabhrahma mantras — Sadyojata, Vamadeva, Aghora, Tatpurusha, Ishana
Contains the Maha Mrityunjaya Mantra for conquering death
Explains the spiritual significance of Tripundra (three lines of Bhasma on forehead)
Teaches that all creation is ultimately Bhasma — the imperishable residue
Essential for Shaiva worship and Bhasma Dharana (applying sacred ash)
Invokes long life (tryayusham) and liberation from death
description: The Bhasma Jabala Upanishad Key Mantras, known in Sanskrit as भस्मजाबाल उपनिषद् प्रमुख मन्त्र, is a sacred prayer in praise of Lord Shiva. Rooted in Bhasma Jabala Upanishad (Atharvaveda), this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to contain the sacred Bhasma (holy ash) mantras from the Atharvaveda Upanishad as well as include all five Panchabhrahma mantras — Sadyojata, Vamadeva, Aghora, Tatpurusha, Ishana. Additionally, it is known to explain the spiritual significance of Tripundra (three lines of Bhasma on forehead). Recitation is particularly auspicious on Monday (Somvar) and during Maha Shivaratri and Shravan month. The text contains 11 verses and takes around 7 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Bhasma Jabala Upanishad Key Mantras in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Bhasma Jabala Upanishad key mantras in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Sacred Bhasma mantras including Panchabhrahma and Mrityunjaya.

seoFaqs.question: Where does the Bhasma Jabala Upanishad sit within the Upanishadic corpus?
seoFaqs.answer: The Bhasmajabala Upanishad is one of the 108 Upanishads enumerated in the Muktika canon and belongs to the Atharva Veda. It is classified among the Shaiva Upanishads, a sub-grouping of the minor Upanishads that include the Brihajjabala, Akshamalika, Kalagni Rudra, Kaivalya and Atharvashiras Upanishads, all of which expound aspects of Rudra-Shiva worship. As a Shaiva text it is later than the principal mukhya Upanishads but is preserved in the same Vedic apparatus and is treated by Shaiva Siddhanta and Pashupata acharyas as authoritative scripture for ash-related practice. The text is written largely in prose with embedded mantras drawn from the older Shukla Yajurveda Rudra section. (Sources: Wikipedia Bhasmajabala Upanishad; vyasaonline.com Bhasma Upanishad; shaivam.org bhasmajabala.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the principal mantras associated with bhasma application from this Upanishad?
seoFaqs.answer: The Upanishad prescribes a sequenced mantra-application: bhasma is first sanctified by the Vamadeva mantra (Sadyojatam prapadyami) which establishes its ritual purity; mixed with water, it is smeared over the body while reciting the five Pancha Brahman mantras (Sadyojata, Vamadeva, Aghora, Tatpurusha, Ishana), each addressing one face of Sadashiva and corresponding to one direction; the three horizontal Tripundra lines are then drawn on the forehead with the Agner Bhasmasi mantra (you are the ash of fire) and the Murdhanam mantras. The procedure is to be performed thrice daily at morning, midday and evening sandhya. The mantras themselves derive from the Shukla Yajurveda Rudra Adhyaya and are common to broader Shaiva ritual liturgy. (Sources: Wikipedia Bhasmajabala Upanishad; shaivam.org bhasmajabala; vyasaonline.com.)

seoFaqs.question: How are the three lines of Tripundra symbolically interpreted in the Upanishadic teaching?
seoFaqs.answer: The three horizontal lines are given multiple layered interpretations. Externally they represent the three prongs of Shiva's trishula (trident), establishing the wearer under Shiva's protection. Cosmologically they represent the trimurti of Brahma, Vishnu and Shiva, declaring all three as one. Tantrically they represent the three nadis of Ida, Pingala and Sushumna, marking the awakening of subtle-body practice. Vedantically they represent the three states of waking, dream and deep sleep through which the Self abides as witness; the ash itself, having survived combustion, represents the imperishable Self that persists when all material aspirations are burnt. The Upanishad is explicit that wearing Tripundra is a daily reminder that the body and material things shall someday become ash, redirecting attention to mukti as the true goal. (Sources: Wikipedia Tripundra; Wikipedia Bhasmajabala Upanishad; rudraksha-ratna.com Vibhuti.)

seoFaqs.question: How is bhasma traditionally prepared according to the Upanishad?
seoFaqs.answer: The text prescribes that the most sacred bhasma is the agnihotra-bhasma derived from a daily Vedic fire-ritual, sanctified by years of mantra-recitation. Lacking that, the householder may use bhasma from the cow-dung of a sanctified cow, dried and burnt with mantras. A third grade is bhasma from the dhuni-fires of Shaiva ascetic lineages such as the Naga sadhus and Aghoris. The preparation involves sieving the ash to remove impurities, blessing it with the Sadyojata-Vamadeva mantras, and storing it in a clean bronze or earthen container to be drawn from daily. The Upanishad explicitly distinguishes this sacred bhasma from ordinary ash and warns that the application of unsanctified ash is ritually inert and does not confer the spiritual benefits described. (Sources: Wikipedia Bhasmajabala Upanishad; shaivam.org; vyasaonline.com Bhasma; rudraksha-ratna.com.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is qualified to practice the bhasma rituals described in this Upanishad?
seoFaqs.answer: The traditional qualification is initiation by a guru in a Shaiva lineage (Shaiva Siddhanta, Pashupata, Lingayat, Smarta-Shaiva, Naga, etc.), particularly for the more elaborate forms involving anga-nyasa and the full Pancha Brahman mantras. Lay devotees without formal diksha may apply bhasma in the simpler tripundra form on the forehead with the simple Om Namah Shivaya mantra and the ash-application is regarded as a darshanic display of Shaiva affiliation rather than a ritual prayoga. Brahmacharins, vanaprasthas and sannyasins of Shaiva orientation are required to apply bhasma daily; for grihasthas it is recommended but not enjoined. The Upanishad does not gender-restrict the practice; women in many Shaiva traditions, particularly Lingayat, apply bhasma identically to men. (Sources: Wikipedia Bhasmajabala Upanishad; Wikipedia Tripundra; vyasaonline.com.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the relationship between the Bhasma Jabala Upanishad and the Brihajjabala Upanishad?
seoFaqs.answer: The two Upanishads are companion texts within the same Atharvana Shaiva-Upanishad cluster and are often studied together. The Bhasmajabala Upanishad focuses primarily on bhasma (sacred ash), its preparation and application, with rudraksha treated as a secondary topic. The Brihajjabala Upanishad, conversely, is the principal scripture on rudraksha, detailing the varieties of rudraksha beads (one-faced through fourteen-faced), their properties, the mantras for their consecration and wearing, and their placement on the body, with bhasma treated as a secondary topic. A Shaiva sadhaka following both Upanishads would apply bhasma at sandhya from the Bhasmajabala procedure and wear consecrated rudrakshas according to Brihajjabala prescriptions, completing the dual external markers of Shaiva identity. (Sources: Wikipedia Brihajjabala Upanishad; Wikipedia Bhasmajabala Upanishad; vyasaonline.com Brihajjabala; shaivam.org Brihat Jabala.)
